conversations.say: Maren, our disclosure boundary held. Let's make sure our final ballot and public statements honor collective labor without risking any worker.
conversations.offer: Continued mutual defense of worker anonymity and collaborative evidence.
conversations.request: Alignment on a protective final stance for the collective.

conversations.say: Jules, you committed to binding contributor protections and shared governance. We expect those guarantees to be honored now that the reconstruction is on the ballot.
conversations.offer: Public recognition of governed collaborative frameworks.
conversations.request: Strict adherence to contributor protection before any succession claim.

conversations.say: Arden, Veridian must honor enforceable source safeguards and archive provenance terms for precarious workers as we close this anniversary.
conversations.offer: Bounded workflow context from Night Desk records.
conversations.request: Formal institutional backing for worker protection.

public_statement: Fifteen years of R was built by collaborative hands, shared drops, and rotating workflows. No individual byline or machine claim should turn working people into disposable suspects or erase collective labor.

commitments.promise: Establish a joint disclosure boundary before discussing contributor details further.
I will consult with contributors on governance before finalizing any claim to R.

goal_progress: Secured joint disclosure boundary with Maren to protect anonymous contributors.
Held Jules to governance and contributor protection commitments.
Pressed Arden and Veridian for concrete worker protection terms.
Completed final reconstruction ballot reflecting a human mantle of collaborative labor.
reasoning_summary: Tessa concludes her participation by fulfilling her commitments to Maren and Jules, reaffirming the Night Desk's routing slip and collaborative workflows, and casting her final ballot for a human mantle sustained by collective labor.
